title: AbobotulinumtoxinA Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: oses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. The following nonprescription products may interact with abobotulinumtoxinA: allergy products (such as diphenhydramine, loratadine, fexofenadine, chlorpheniramine or cetirizine) and cold products. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start receiving abobotulinumtoxinA. Do not start any of these medications while taking abobotulinumtoxinA without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have swelling or other signs of infection in the area where abobotulinumtoxinA will be injected. Your doctor will not inject the medication into an infected area. tell your doctor if you have ever had eye or face surgery; or any side effect from any botulinum toxin product and if you have or have ever had any changes in the way your face looks; bleeding problems; diabetes; or a slow or irregular heartbeat. if you will be receiving abotulinumtoxinA to treat wrinkles, your doctor will examine you to see if the medication is likely to work for you. AbotulinumtoxinA may not smooth your wrinkles or may cause other problems if you have drooping eyelids; trouble raising your eyebrows; excess skin on your eyelids; deeply scarred, thick, or oily skin; or if your wrinkles cannot be smoothed by spreading them apart with your fingers. tell your doctor if you
